questionHtml: Calculate the enthalpy change of reaction, ΔH, in kJ, for the decomposition of calcium carbonate.
markschemeHtml:
«ΔH =» (−635 «kJ» – 393.5 «kJ») – (−1207 «kJ») ✓

«ΔH = + » 179 «kJ» ✓


Award [2] for correct final answer.

Award [1 max] for −179 kJ.

Ignore an extra step to determine total enthalpy change in kJ: 179 kJ mol-1 x 5.55 mol = 993 kJ.

Award [2] for an answer in the range 990 - 993« kJ».

meta.title: Calculate the enthalpy change of reaction, ΔH, in kJ, for the decomposition of calcium carbonate.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.2.3—Standard enthalpy changes of combustion, ΔHc⦵, and formation, ΔHf⦵, data are used in thermodynamic calculations. Deduce equations and solutions to problems involving these terms.
